title: Tontelea calypsoides
intro: Tontelea calypsoides, commonly known as the "Calypso Vine," is a climbing plant native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The Calypso Vine has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tontelea calypsoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the Calypso Vine is often made into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
